ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for garlic prawn skewers; closest parent 'shrimp food product' (FOODON_03302051) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CJW covers shrimps and prawns as a category; no specific code for garlic-marinated grilled prawn skewers exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227144002 identifies prawn as a substance; no specific concept for garlic prawn skewers exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 5 prawn skewers, approximately 150 g, as typically sold at a Chinese restaurant or hawker stall in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Shrimp, mixed species, cooked, moist heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15152)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp (NDB 15152 / FDC 175180). Values adjusted upward for garlic marinade and oil coating typical of Chinese-style garlic prawn skewers: fat_g increased from ~1.1 to ~5.5 g per 100 g to account for oil used in marination and grilling; sodium_mg increased from ~224 to ~480 mg per 100 g to account for seasoning salt; energy_kcal recalculated accordingly. Protein and carbohydrate values retained from USDA base. This is an adapted estimate; exact restaurant formulation is unknown.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for garlic prawn skewers. Prawns and other shellfish are high-protein, very low-carbohydrate foods. Per the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature, cooked shrimp and similar crustaceans have a GI of approximately 5 (low), consistent with the negligible carbohydrate content (~2 g per 100 g). GL per 150 g serving = (5 × 3.0) / 100 = 0.15, rounded to 0.2. GI set to 5 as the closest defensible analogous estimate for a high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled seafood product.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from seasoning and marinade
hypertension.guidance: Each 150 g serving provides approximately 720 mg sodium, representing about 31% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients should limit portion size and request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 3 g of carbohydrate per 150 g serving and a GI of 5, garlic prawn skewers have a negligible glycemic load and are suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as part of a balanced meal, provided sodium intake is monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Prawns are naturally high in dietary cholesterol (~228 mg per 150 g serving) and the sodium content from seasoning is elevated; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation and balance with low-sodium, high-fibre foods in the same meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Garlic prawn skewers (5 pieces) is a Chinese-style grilled seafood dish consisting of whole prawns marinated in garlic, oil, salt, and seasoning, then threaded onto skewers and grilled or barbecued until cooked through. The dish is popular in Chinese restaurants and hawker-style eateries in Singapore, where it is commonly served as a starter, side dish, or sharing plate. The prawns develop a lightly charred exterior with a juicy, tender interior, and the garlic marinade imparts a pungent, savoury aroma. The dish is high in protein (~20 g per 100 g), low in carbohydrates (~2 g per 100 g), and moderate in fat (~5.5 g per 100 g) due to the oil used in marination and grilling. It is a good source of selenium, vitamin B12, phosphorus, and cholesterol. The glycemic index is very low (approximately 5), making it suitable for blood sugar management. The primary nutritional concern is sodium, which is elevated due to seasoning, making portion control advisable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and is suitable for halal diets when prepared with halal-certified ingredients.
common_user_queries: How many calories in garlic prawn skewers?
Is prawn skewer high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp data with adjustments for garlic marinade and oil; no exact match for garlic prawn skewers exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or other primary regional sources. GI is an analogous estimate based on the low-carbohydrate profile of cooked crustaceans per peer-reviewed GI literature.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp (FDC 175180) with upward adjustments for oil and seasoning typical of Chinese-style garlic prawn skewers. Exact restaurant formulation from Lan Ting Xu Lanzhou Beef Noodles is unknown. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked shrimp and is not reliably established for this preparation. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source for cooked shrimp; no trans fats expected from plant oil marinade). Serving size of 150 g is estimated for 5 medium-to-large prawns on skewers; actual weight may vary by restaurant. GI of 5 is an analogous estimate for a high-protein, very low-carbohydrate grilled crustacean product, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database values for similar seafood items.

improvement_actions_taken: Canonical food name normalized from 'garlic flavored prawn skewers (5pcs)' to 'Garlic Prawn Skewers (5 pieces)'.
Multilingual local names added for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singaporean-served Chinese cuisine.
Nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy FDC 175180 (cooked shrimp) with documented adaptation for garlic oil marinade and seasoning salt.
GI set to 5 (low) as analogous estimate for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled crustacean per Atkinson et al. 2021 AJCN peer-reviewed GI tables.
Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1 with explicit documentation in notes as required by g3 sentinel values rule.
Trans_fat_g set to 0.0 with confirmed zero justification in notes.
